response: To solve this problem we first choose three distinct rows out of the six available rows which can be done in 20 ways. Next we choose three distinct columns out of the six available columns which also yields 20 ways. Finally we place the three rooks in the intersection of these chosen rows and columns such that no two share a row or column which is equivalent to finding a bijection between the three chosen rows and the three chosen columns giving us 6 possible arrangements. Multiplying these values together gives 20 times 20 times 6 which equals 2400.
